title: The Pomo
summary: The Pomo led by Essie Parrish are a custom civilization mod by Coiot and Orangechrisy, with contributions from DarthKyofu.

civilization.title: The Pomo
civilization.body: The Pomo have been living Northern California for thousands of years, estimates suggesting they moved into the area around 4000-7000 BCE. Though another theory suggests a Pomo group pushed out the existing people in the region between 500 BCE and 1300 CE, when the lands became more settled and developed. Trade grew and would reach across California, with materials from the Channel Islands in Southern California being found in the region, and the thousands of charmstones at Tolay Lake including some that came from as far away as Mexico. At some point the shamanistic Kuksu religion developed and spread across the region. Kuksu is most noted among the Pomo and their neighboring Patwin, and utilized elaborate dances, regalia, and subterranean dance rooms. Kuksu spread from the Pomo and Patwin across much of Northern and Central California.

leader.title: Essie Parrish
leader.body: Essie Pinola Parrish was a Kashaya Pomo Dreamer, basket weaver, teacher, and activist. Born in 1902, Parrish was raised by her grandmother Rosie Jarvis who was also a great tribal historian. At an early age she was recognized as a shaman, and was soon considered a prophet and Dreamer. In 1943, the previous religious leader of the Kashaya Pomo died and Parrish became the official leader, she would also go on to teach the last Pomo Dreamer, Mabel McKay how to lead. She would lead religious dances and the community as a Dreamer, helping to teach the younger generation in the Kashaya Pomo language and push people away from losing their Indian blood. She also raised 16 children with her husband Sidney Parrish. Throughout the mid 1900s she also engaged in activism, advocating in testimonies to the government for her community. She also would work with ethnographers and linguists to preserve the Kashaya Pomo language and traditions, helping to create over 20 films one of which one the 1866 Western Heritage Award. Essie Parrish has been remembered in a few books such as The Roots of Our Culture/Stories of Essie Parrish, and in Mabel McKay: Weaving the Dream.

dawnOfMan.blessing:
Essie Parrish, you led your people through both the physical and spiritual turmoil of the 20th century. Your people had suffered greatly under the heel of the American Empire, so in 1943, when you became the spiritual leader of the Pomo, the path forward was harsh. But you rejected the potential destruction of your people and instead pushed back, bringing your community together and ensuring a lasting cultural legacy. Your leadership, basketry, and ethnographic films helped to give the Pomo safety and protection, and your teachings allowed future generations to continue the traditions.
Essie, your people come to you once more. They need a Dreamer to guide them safely forward through another treacherous time. Will you be able to ensure the legacy of the Pomo once again? Can you secure your culture and your people from the threats around them? Can you build a civilization that will stand the test of time?
dawnOfMan.introduction: Camay, I knew you were coming. Now come this way, I have a basket saved for you. Then we discuss other matters
dawnOfMan.defeat: I've done what I could to preserve our culture, so we will not be gone forever. Our ghosts will return as your plague is cleansed from these lands. The Dream never lies.

uniques.name: Spirits of the Four Directions
uniques.body: Gain a different bonus depending on the direction of the last converted city.
bullets: South: Units heal +10 HP when healing.
East: +20% Great Person Great People generation.
North: At the start of the turn, Religious units give nearby units +1 Moves Movement.
West: Trade Route Trade Routes in Cities give +5% Food Growth to that city.

uniques.name: Basket Weaver
bullets: Creates the Basket unique Great Work, providing a different bonus to the capital depending on the direction of the city the Great Work is in.

uniques.name: Roundhouse
bullets: +1 Faith Faith for every 4 Citizens Citizens in the city.
Has a Great Work Great Work of Art slot,
+10% Religious Pressure from this City per Great Work Great Work in it.
